title: Onboarding Playbook
values: Greet new hires by name, share the team mission in one sentence, and let them know what the first week looks like.
Walk through the founding story, key milestones, and the core values that guide every decision.
Show the org chart, point out key leads, and explain how teams collaborate across functions.
Review the job description, 30/60/90 day targets, and how success is measured in this role.
Get set up on day one
Walk through the tool stack—email, Slack, Notion, Github—and confirm all access is provisioned.
Explain response time SLAs, meeting culture, and how decisions get documented.
Cover password policies, 2FA requirements, data handling rules, and how to report incidents.
Surface what employees are entitled to
Review health, dental, PTO, learning budget, and how to enroll or make changes.
Introduce the onboarding buddy program—who their buddy is, weekly check-in cadence, and what to ask.
Share the detailed calendar for the first week including orientation calls, team intros, and ramp tasks.
Point to the internal wiki, HR handbook, IT help desk, and the new-hire FAQ doc.
Give a clear call to action
Summarise the top three actions the new hire should complete before end of day and who to contact with questions.
